scope: Pass B full-surface expansion: every Broker-API-specific endpoint documented at docs.alpaca.markets (the reference index at docs.alpaca.markets/llms.txt) is enumerated below. 11 read streams now cover accounts, assets, market calendar/clock/country info, account activities, journals, and per-account positions/watchlists/orders/document-metadata (the last 4 via the engine's fan_out dialect, resolving account ids from the accounts list). This connector remains read-only (capabilities.write: false): trading/funding/journal-creation/account-opening/KYC-update endpoints all move money, place trades, or mutate compliance-sensitive account state, none of which is a safe reverse-ETL write target for a metadata/reporting connector. Endpoints under Alpaca's separate Trading API / Market Data API product lines (stock bars/quotes/trades, news, corporate actions, fixed income, indices, logos, screener) are a distinct product surface from the Broker API this connector targets and are out of scope entirely, not individually enumerated below.

excluded.reason: opens a brand-new brokerage account with full KYC/identity/contact submission; account-opening is an onboarding workflow action, not a reverse-ETL data write

single-account detail by id returns the identical full AccountExtended shape the accounts list stream already returns per account (Alpaca's own docs confirm GET /v1/accounts returns the complete AccountExtended object, not a summary) -- no additional data over the accounts stream
mutates KYC identity/contact/beneficiary/compliance-disclosure data on an existing account; too high-risk/compliance-sensitive for an unattended reverse-ETL write

trading-detail view of a single account overlaps the accounts stream's own fields (currency, buying power, equity); not enough unique data over the accounts list to justify a dedicated fan_out stream in this pass
Alpaca's own docs mark this endpoint deprecated, sunsetting 2026-07-06, superseded by FINRA Rule 4210's intraday margin regime
removes a Pattern Day Trader flag from an account, a compliance-status change with regulatory implications
returns a binary image (asset logo), not a JSON list/detail resource
single-asset detail by symbol/id; the assets stream already lists every asset's full object in one unpaginated response, so this adds no new data
per-asset regulatory entry-requirement metadata for a narrow BETA options-trading workflow, not part of the core metadata surface this connector targets
BETA options-contract catalog; a distinct, still-evolving sub-product not yet stable enough to commit a schema against

Alpaca's own docs mark this legacy corporate-actions-announcements endpoint deprecated in favor of the newer corporate actions API, and it requires a mandatory 90-day-max since/until date range this connector's static config model has no natural place for
redirects to a pre-signed URL serving the actual document (PDF/binary); the documents stream already covers the JSON list/detail metadata (id, type, date, name)
uploads a binary owner document (multipart/base64 payload); the dialect's write-action body model targets structured JSON/form records, not document uploads
lists linked bank-account ACH relationships (routing/account-number-adjacent banking PII); deferred funding-surface expansion, not implemented in this pass
links a new external bank account to a brokerage account for ACH funding; a funding-instrument-creation action, not a routine data write
unlinks an existing ACH funding relationship
lists linked wire-transfer recipient banks (banking PII); deferred funding-surface expansion, matching the ACH-relationships exclusion above
registers a new wire-transfer recipient bank for an account; funding-instrument creation
removes a registered recipient bank
lists ACH/wire money-movement transfers for an account; deferred funding-surface expansion alongside ACH relationships/recipient banks above
initiates a real money transfer into or out of a brokerage account
cancels a pending money transfer, an irreversible action on real funds movement
moves funds or securities between accounts; not a safe reverse-ETL write target
creates a one-to-many batch journal, moving funds/securities across many accounts at once; higher blast radius than a single journal
cancels a pending journal entry moving funds/securities
IPO offering catalog; a distinct product surface (new-issue underwriting data) unrelated to this connector's core account/asset/market metadata scope
single-IPO detail by id; not implemented since the list endpoint itself is out of scope above
OAuth client metadata lookup for Alpaca's end-user OAuth login flow, not an account/asset/market data resource
OAuth authorization-code issuance; an authentication-flow endpoint, not a data mutation
OAuth token exchange; an authentication-flow endpoint, not a data mutation
places a live trade order; moves real money/positions, the highest-risk mutation this API exposes
replaces (modifies) an existing live order's price/qty
cancels a single open order
cancels every open order for an account in one call
single-order detail by id; the orders stream already lists every order (status=all) per account
single-order lookup by client order id; same duplicate-of-orders-stream reasoning as getorderforaccount
single-position detail by symbol/asset id; the positions stream already lists every open position per account
closes (liquidates) a single open position
closes (liquidates) every open position for an account in one call
Alpaca's own docs mark this bulk-positions endpoint deprecated in favor of GET /v1/reporting/eod/positions
end-of-day positions reporting snapshot across accounts; a bulk reporting export shape distinct from the per-account positions stream already covered, deferred to a future pass
end-of-day cash interest reporting export; deferred reporting-surface expansion
real-time trading-limit metadata for a single account; narrow trading-risk-config detail not yet paired with a dedicated stream
exercises a BETA options position; an irreversible trading action
instructs Alpaca NOT to exercise a BETA options position at expiry; an irreversible trading-instruction action
single-watchlist detail by id; the watchlists stream already lists every watchlist per account
creates a new named watchlist; low-risk but not implemented in this pass alongside the read-only watchlists stream
replaces a watchlist's name/symbol set; not implemented in this pass
permanently removes a watchlist

GIFT City (India-region) blocked-symbols list served from a completely separate regional host (ext-api.giftcity(.sandbox).in.alpaca.markets) with its own Bearer-token customer auth, not this connector's configured Broker API base_url/Basic-auth credential
IRA excess-contribution records; a narrow retirement-account compliance sub-feature not part of the core metadata surface
activities filtered by a single type in the path; the account_activities stream already returns every activity across all types in one unfiltered listing

Server-Sent Events (SSE) streaming subscription for account status changes; a persistent-connection push protocol, not a declarative poll-style list/detail resource the engine's HTTP dialect can express
SSE streaming subscription for trade events; same push-protocol scope narrowing as the account-status events endpoint
SSE streaming subscription for journal-status events; same push-protocol scope narrowing
SSE streaming subscription for transfer-status events; same push-protocol scope narrowing
SSE streaming subscription for non-trading-activity events; same push-protocol scope narrowing
SSE streaming subscription for system-wide events; same push-protocol scope narrowing
